text: Ẹ̀dẹ ọkpa ke dò re.
Ẹ̀kpẹ̀n ne ọba avbe àr̅anmwẹn-òha hia ke dǒ biẹ.
Ùgbẹ̀n vbè ọ ghi bìẹ̀ nẹn, ọ na wẹ :
"Vbè ì r̅a khian r̅u ẹ̀mwẹn èvbàre hẹ̌ vbè ẹ̀ghẹ̀ nâ nè ǐ
Uwuệr̅e nî ọ na yère ighẹ̀ ẹ̀ i mwẹn avbe àr̅amwẹn-òha man
Ọ na ghì yǎn ighẹ̀ ir̅àn ẹ̀r̅' ir̅ẹ̀n ghi yà kòk' ọ̀mon, kòk'
Ọ̀finotọ̀ gbǎ ẹkpèn sẹ̀ vberìọ̀,
Ọ̀ ghi wà họn igh' ẹ̀kpẹ̀n biẹè, ọ na kp' ǒtọ̀ kp' ǒtọ̀
Ọ̀ ghi gùà làdìàn nẹn, ọ na zẹ̀ ekẹ̀n kherhe gù' ùvun nî.
As soon as he had come out at the tiger's floor, he covered his hole with a layer of sand,
and then hid quietly in it, 1istening to hear
Ọ̀ ghi wà họn ighẹ̀ ẹ̀kpẹ̀n w' ir̅ẹ̀n gha y' avb' ar̅amwẹ̀n-
Ọ na si avb' âr̅amwẹn-òha hia kòko.

Òhan ke ghì mù ir̅àn ẹ̀sẹsè .
Ir̅àn na gha ha yo ha re.
Uw' ẹr̅ě nî ọ̀finotọ̀ ghi nà wệ :
"Wà ghẹ gì' òhan m' uwà; òdẹ rọọ̀ n' à gha l' ẹkpẹ̌n r̅è."
not to be afraid.
He advised them that when they take their gifts to the tiger, they should not enter his house,
Wà ghà ghì khàmǎn r̅ện nẹn, wà sẹ̀tin lẹ̌."
Iràn hia kêghì ghọ̀ghọ̀ y' ẹ̀wàẹ̀n n' ọ̀fǐnotọ̀ zẹè.
Ọ̀ man ghi he kpẹe, èdọmwândọghẹe na gb' ìkun-erhan

"Gha ọ kha ọ̀r̅e?''

Ọ na sùẹ̀n gha yan èyan hia khệ ọ̀finotọ̀, bhen' ìr̅ẹ̀n
Vb' uw' ẹghẹ̌ nâ, ọ̀finotọ̀ vbe lẹ̀r̅è y' ùvùn ẹr̅ẹ̀n nẹ̀n sin.
Ọ̀ r̅ẹ̌nr̅ện igh' ẹ̀ i mwẹn ẹ̀kpẹ̀n man z' ir̅ò n' ọ̀ khọ̌ da
Ọ na fẹ̀ko gha damwẹ̀n-èhọ̀ vb' uw' uvùn ẹr̅ẹ̀n.
Ọ̀ họn èyan hia n' ẹ̀kpẹ̀n yan kh' ọr̅è .
Ọ̀ ghi gie avb' ar̅amwẹn-òha hia y' ọgh' ẹkpẹ̀ǹ gbà`
Ọ na gha dì' òdar̅' ow' ẹkpẹ̀n.
Ọ̀ ghi s' òbọ dẹ̀dẹ̀dẹ̀ nẹn, ọ na dà khàmàn ẹkpện n' ọ

Ẹ̀kpẹ̀n ghi kìè urhô nẹn, gìẹ̀ gha vin yan oọ̀finotọ̀,
Èkpèn na ghì we èwàèn r̅èn gha ya r̅u 'rè.
Ẹ̀rẹ ọ ghi nà gha go tie ọ̀ finotọ̀ n' ọ wěrìègbe gha
O na we ofinoto man ren ighe t' iren te khian ded'
Ọ̀finotọ̀ na gèlè wèrìègbe gha dê.
Ẹ̀kpẹ̀n man r̅ẹn igh' ộfinotọ̀ z' ir̅o èmwìn n' ọ̀ gha r̅u
Ọ̀ ghi s' èhè n' ẹ̀kpẹ̀n ye nẹn, ẹ̀kpẹ̀n na we n' ọ lâhọ
Ọ̀finotọ̀ na wẹ ọ̀ mǎn.
Ọ̀ ghi gìè ẹ̀kpẹ̀n fi iyekê gbè nẹn, ọ na tọ̀n òvbì ẹr̅è
Ẹ̀kpẹ̀n ghi họ̀n vb' òvbi ẹr̅e da tu, ọ na vìn gha dê ;
When the tiger heard his cub cry, he rushed back;
but before he got to where the rabbit was, the rabbit had
Ùkp' ẹrhùnrhùn ẹr̅ẹ̀n n' òb' ẹkpẹ̀n ghi vbǎ na bòlò y'
Ọ̀ni ọ si ẹ̀r̅e n' ùkpẹrhurhun ọ̀finotọ̀ na bà rìẹ̀rìẹ̀rìẹ̀ sẹ̀
Èvba nî òkha nâ na yǎ dê wù.
Òkha ọkpa ke dǒ rè.
Ọ na yǎ de mù ọ̀vbokhon-òkhùò ọkpa n' à tì ' ẹ̀r̅' Ènàbulelè.
there lived a girl called Enabulele.
Right from the day she was born, she had always been a problem-child.
One day, her mother wanted to go to the farm.
She instructed Enabulele not to leave
N' ọ ghẹ gèlè sẹ̀tin kpaà, ìy' ẹr̅e na rhò òkuta y' ọ̀r̅è
Ọ na we nè Ènàbubele ghẹ kpa hìn òwa re a te miẹn ighẹ̀
Ènàbulele na wẹ̌ hè.
Gi' 'ye Ènàbulele gha z' òwẹ̌ kpa hin òwa ré avb' ọsi
Ènàbulele nâ khàmàn avb ' ọsì ọr̅è igh' iy' ir̅ẹn we
Ọ na wẹ : "Òko, wà dǒ ghe èmwìn n' iye mwẹn mu yọ
Ọ man ga nẹn, òhan i gun mwẹn kpa hin òwa re."
Avb' ọsi Ènàbulele na gèlè yǎ ghe òkuta n' iyẹe mu
Ir̅àn ghi mì ' ọrẹ̀n nẹn, ọkpâ vb' ir̅ân na w' ir̅ẹ̀n r̅ẹn
Ọ na rhùlẹ̀ gha ri ' ìyek' owa.
Ọ na yǎ gb ' eb' iyôkho re;
Ọ na vi' ọr̅è yàn òkuta ni
Ọ na kàkàbọ si erhện y ' àkhe.
Ọ̀ ghi kpẹe kherhe, iràn na w' ẹ̀ger̅e ghě.
Ènàbulele ghi y' obọ kan r̅ẹn, ọ̀ wa ye khùẹ̀rhẹ̀khùẹ̀rhẹ̀.
Uwẹre nî ìhùa 're na zẹ̀giẹgiẹ wệ: "Ènàbubele, man
Gì' a gha rì' òh' okpàghà nian."
Ènàbulele na gèlè lèlè ir̅àn.
Iràn ghi zẹ khian, ir̅an na dǒ s' ẹzẹ̀.
Ẹ̀zẹ̀ nî, à ghà vè ọ̀r̅è èmwin, ọ ghî wà eva n' ọ̀mwǎn
Sòkpan degh' a man rhi ' èmwìn n' a v' ẹzě r̅e n' ẹzè,
Ènàbulele vb ' avb' ọsi ộrè ghi s ' ọ̀kpen ẹ̀zẹ̀, èdaa-
Ẹ̀zẹ̀ na gèlè wà n' ir̅àn gberà.
Ir̅àn ghi rhọ̀ òkpaghà fò nẹn, ir̅àn na z' òd' owà.
When they had finished picking cassia pods, they set out for home.
As they got to the river, They each threw
N' ọ rhirhi fi ọghẹe fì ẹzẹ̀, ẹ̀zẹ̀ ghi wa nẹn n' ọ
Iràn hia na dǒ gberà; ọ̀ na kẹ Èǹabulele ọkpa.
Ìhùa 'r̅e na k' òbọ̀ n' ọ kẹr̅e vàn khàmǎn r̅ẹn n' ọ
Sòkpan Èǹàbulele man damwẹn-èhọ ir̅àn.
Sẹriọ ọ̀ r̅ẹnr̅ẹn ighẹ̀ degh' ìr̅ẹ̀n man rhi' òkpàghǎ fi
Sòkpan rhumwûnda n' ọ̀ na r̅' igbǎkhùàn, ọ̀ man dâmwẹ̀n-
Ọ̀ ghi zẹ kpẹe, ọ na z' udù là uwu ẹ̀zẹ̀ .
and stubbornly stepped into the river.
She thought she could cross it without its
Ọ̀ man he k' ọ̌r̅e yà z' òw' èha vb' ẹ̀zẹ̌ suẹn gha r̅o
Ìhua 'r̅e ghi ghè vb' ẹ̀zẹ̀ r̅o s' ọ̀re igbọn, ir̅an na z'
Ènàbulele rhì' okpǎghà fì ẹzẹ̀, rhì' okpǎghà fì ẹzẹ̀
Vben' ir̅an s' ihuan nẹ sẹ, ọ̀ man rhi' òkpàghǎ fî ẹzẹ̀.
In spite of how long they sang, Enabulele did not throw the cassia pod into the river.
The river continued to rise round her.
It soon rose as high as her waist line.
Èsò vb' ir̅an na ghì r̅hùlẹ̀ yǎ ti' iy' ẹr̅e rè.
Iy' ẹr̅e ghi tè re, ẹ̀zẹ̀ r̅o s' Ènàbulele ẹ̀wẹe nẹ̀n.
When she came and found that the water had risen to her daughter's chest,
she started to cry, and sang
the same the same song Enabulele's friends
Ènàbulele man yě damwẹn-èhọ iy' ẹr̅è .
Ẹ̀zẹ̀ na yè gha ro gu ẹ̀r̅ě khian.
Iy' ẹr̅e man ghi r̅ện vb' ọ̀ gha r̅û.
Ọ na ghi wệ : "Ọ̀ mǎn, ì gha ya tì' ìgbàkhìàn r̅ẹn re.
Ù ghǎ ghe ọ̀ gha dàmwẹ̀n-ehọ ọnî."
Iy' ẹr̅e na gèlè yǎ tî' ìgbàkhìàn r̅ện re .
Ọ̀ ghi re, ọ na vbè z' ìhuan so :
Ènàbulele rhi' okpǎghà fì' ẹzẹ̀, rhì' okpǎghà fi ẹzẹ̀
Vben' ọ s' ihuan sẹ̀, Ènàbulele man rhi' èhọ nẹn tǎ yi.
By this time, the water had risen up to her neck.
Ọ na wệ : "Ì gha ya gb' adẹn n' i do yǎ sùà òkpàghǎ
Ọ na gèlè zẹ̀giẹgiẹ yǎ gb' adẹn n' ọ taẹ̀n rè .
Ọ na ghì yè n' adẹn rùà ìkun òkpàghà n' Ènàbulele mu
Ọ̀wàr̅ọkpa nî, ẹ̀zẹ̌ na ghì wà y' ihi' èva n' Ènàbulele
Èvbǎ nî òkha nâ na yǎ dê wù.
Òkha ọkpa ke dǒ re.
Ọ na yǎ de mù ègui vb' emwan hia n' ì re ẹvbǒ nî.
there lived a tortoise and all the people of the town.
A severe famine broke out in that town.
The tortoise was too lazy to farm.
Finally when starvation was getting the better of him,
Ẹ̀d' ẹkì ghi re, ọ na rhì' òvbi èmàn;
Ọ na fẹ̀ko yǎ lẹr̅e y' òvbi òdẹ n' èkhện ẹ̀kì la gbera.
and went and hid carefully near the footpath which market-people take to the market.
Ẹ̀kì na dǒ r̅o.
Ẹ̀kì ghi rò nẹn, ègui na z' ìhuan so vb' èhè n' ọ
Ẹ̀ki ogìso, kpuman; n' ọ man gha rie, kpuman; èmwìn n'
Ọ na gha sò ẹ̀r̅ě yan ègbe.
Òhan na gha mu èkhẹn ẹ̀ki hia.
Ir̅àn r̅ǒ igh' ẹ̀r̅imwin ọ̀ kpě deè.
Èhia na mù vẹ.
Ir̅an na lẹ̀ s' èmwin ir̅an hia r̅àe y' ẹkì.
Ègui ghi gi' ir̅an hia kpâ nẹn, ọ na yàǹkàn làdìàn vb'
Ọ na yǎ kok' èmwìn hia n èkhẹ̌n lẹ sẹ̀rae.
and went and quickly collected all the things the women had left behind,
and took them home.
At last, after several days of hunger,
Ọ na wẹ, "Àh, ọ̀nà i ghi gha r̅u!"

Èkhẹ̀n hia na vbè dǒ gha la gberâ.
Ọ̀ ghi vbè gi' iran hia gbera fò nẹn, ọ na vbè z'
Ẹ̀ki ògiso, kpuman; ẹ̀ki ògiso , kpuman; n' ọ man gha
Èkhẹn ẹ̀ki ghi vbè hìn 'huan nî, ir̅an hia na vbè sùẹ̀n
Ọ te s' ifunàrò èva, èhe hia vbe ye gbẹlẹ nẹ̀n.
Sòkpan ọ man ke ùk' ọba yǎ lẹ̀ hìn ẹki rè fǒ vb' ègui
Ùk' ọba ghi bẹ̀ghè ègui, ọ na wệ: "ẹ̀r̅' i kha ighẹ̀
Ìwina èmwìn n' à viọ mu ègui, ọ̀ man bẹgh' ǔk' ọba.
Ọni fẹ̀ko kọ̀n hìn ẹki rè.
Ọ na zẹ̀giẹgiẹ gha ri' eguae.
Ọ̀ ghi s' ẹguae, ọ na khàmàn avb' eghàẹ̀vbọ̀ n' ir̅an
Iran na gèlè rhi' ẹr̅e gìè ọba.
Ọ̀ ghi s' evba, ọ na wệ : "Dọ' mọn n' ọ yaẹn mwẹ̀n n'
Èmwìn n' àr̅ò mwẹn miẹn ẹ̀r̅' i we n' i do khamàn r̅ùẹn.
he narrated to the king what he had seen;
that it wasn't an evil spirit that came to frighten the people away from the market,
Ọ̀ man kẹ mwẹn ya lẹ̀ hin ẹki re fo vbè ọ ghar̅a de
Àrò mwẹn 'vèva nâ ẹ̀r̅' i ya bẹ̀gh' ẹr̅è."

Ọ na gìè nǎ tie avb' owinà ẹ̀giẹgìẹ̀.
Ọ na we n' ir̅an kà ọ̀mwan, n' ir̅an y' ọ̀dan sìkàn r̅ẹ̀n
Ọ na wệ: "Ẹ̀d' ẹkì n' ọ̀ dě ghà re, wǎ ghi yǎ muẹn y'
Avb' owîna na gèlè kà ọ̀mwǎn nî vben' ọba khamàn iràn.
The carpenters carved the figure as they were instructed.
On the next market day, they went and placed it
Ègui ghi vbè gi' ẹ̀ki r̅ô nẹn, ọ na vbè sùẹ̀n gha sû ìhuan.
When the market was full, the tortoise again started to sing.
All the people in the market fled as usual.
However, the figure carved by the carpenters remained at the middle of the market.
After waiting for a while for it to go, and it didn't go,
Ọ̀ ghi s' èhè n' ọ̀ ye, ọ na we n' ìr̅ẹ̌n gb' ubi y'
Ọ na ghi we n' ir̅ẹ̌n y' obọ n' ọ kẹr̅e sua 'r̅e, òbọ̀ r̅ě
Ọ na we n' ir̅ẹn y' owẹ suẹe, òwẹ̀ or̅e na vbè sìkàn muẹn.
Then he decided to kick it, and his leg got glued to it.
Finally he tried pushing it down with his body, but again his
Vben' ọ ghi zùgh' ègbe sẹ, ọ̀ man sẹtin z' ègbe hìn
Uwẹr̅ě nî ọba na gì' evban èso n' ir̅an ya gh' ẹr̅e ghě
Ir̅an ghi dǒ vba, ègui gha gù' utukpumwẹrhan zugh'
Ir̅an na yǎ khaman ọba èmwìn n' ir̅an miẹ̀n.
Ọba na tì' ìk' ẹvbò ẹ̀giẹgìẹ̀.
Ọ na we n' èmwan eso ya mu ègui rè.
Ir̅an ghi muẹn r̅è nẹn, o na n' ẹ̌vbo vben' ir̅an hǒ n'
Ir̅an hia na we t' a gha gb' ẹ̀r̅ě r̅ua.
Ọba na vbe n' ẹvbo vben' ir̅an hǒ n' a ya gb' ègui
Ir̅an na we àmẹn èrhẹ̀n ẹ̀r̅' a gha mùẹn fi.
They answered that he should be be thrown into hot water.
Immediately, they boiled water,
and as soon as they threw the tortoise into it,
he started to stretch with the heat.
He was dying, but he started to
Ir̅an na wệ: "Èguì vb' u a giẹ yi?"
Ègui na wê: "Wà r̅o ighè t' u wa r̅ù mwen kho n' ù
Ir̅an ghi gèlè ghě, t' ọ ghi tan yộ;
Ir̅an ghi nà zẹ̀giẹgiẹ yǎ mu àmẹn òdìdọ̀n re.
Ir̅an na mù ègui fiọ.
Ir̅an ghi mù ègui fiọ nẹn, ègui na bà hùnwan hir̅ir̅i.
As soon as they had put him in, he remained very still.
The people then assumed that he was dead,
and so they threw him into a near-by bush.
The tortoise waited for everybody to get out of sight,
Ọ na ghi yànkàn gha riè.
Evbǎ nî òkha nâ na yǎ dê mu.
Òkha ọkpa ke dǒ rè.
Ọ na yǎ de mù ọ̀finotọ̀ vb' okpǎghà.
Ir̅an na dǒ gha rộ.
Òwi' owiẹ̀, ọ̀finotọ̀ ghà rhìọ̀re, ọ na làdìàn vb' ùvùn ẹr̅ẹn,
Ọ ghî ghọ̀ghùa.
Ọ ghi rhọ̌ èhia là ùvùn ẹr̅ẹn.
Ẹ̀d' ọkpa ghi re, ọ na wệ: "Òkpàghà na, ọ̀se 'sì wǎ nọ̀."
One day, he said to himself: "This cassia tree,must be a very thoughtful friend!
Even though I'm so far away from him, he sends me
Ì ghà ghì wà b' òwa kẹe ghi vbo?"
Ẹ̀r' ọ ghi na gèlè yǎ tun 'vùn ẹr̅ẹn k' ẹ̀zi okpàghà.
Finally he decided to go and dig his hole right at the foot of the tree.
The next morning he rushed out of his hole
to go and pick the cassia seeds he was sure would be waiting for him,
Ọ̀ ghi ladian, ọ̀ghi ghè èhe hia, èhe hia ye gbẹlẹ.
When he came out, he looked round, and to his amazement, everywhere was bare.
Well, he said he may have come out too early.
So he went back into his hole.
After a while, he crawled out again to
Ọ̀ ghi yè vbè do vba, ès' esò i rọ.
T' ob' ohànmwẹ̀n ghi dae.
Ẹ̀r' ọ ghi na wệ, "T' i gha do werìègbe gha ri èh̀è n'
Ẹ̀r̅' ọ na gèlè wèrìègbe kùn kpà gha ri' uria n' ọ̀
Ọ̀ ghi sè 'vba, ìkp' okpàghà vun èhe hia.
Ọ na ghì zẹ̀giẹgiẹ rhọ̀ ehia vùn uvun ẹr̅ẹn.
Ọ̀ ghi r̅è nẹn, ẹ̀ko vun or̅ẹn, o na wê: "Èr' ir̅an gele
Èvba nî òkha nâ na yǎ de wù.
